function.name: gt_polecat_list
function.description: List all active polecats (worker agents) in a rig, showing their current status and assigned work.

rig.description: The rig name to list polecats for (e.g., 'gastown')

function.name: gt_polecat_nuke
function.description: Kill a polecat session, delete its worktree, and clean up its agent bead. Use --force to override safety checks when a polecat is in a broken state.

target.description: The polecat to nuke, in format 'rig/name' (e.g., 'gastown/furiosa')

force.description: Override safety checks (use when polecat is in broken/zombie state)

function.name: gt_peek
function.description: View the last N lines of a polecat's tmux session output to assess its current state.

target.description: The agent to peek at, in format 'rig/name' (e.g., 'gastown/furiosa')

lines.description: Number of lines to capture from the session

function.name: gt_session_status
function.description: Check the tmux session status of an agent (running/dead, uptime, session ID).

target.description: The agent to check, in format 'rig/name' (e.g., 'gastown/furiosa')

function.name: gt_nudge
function.description: Send a message directly to another agent's Claude Code session via tmux. Use for waking idle agents or requesting action.

message.description: The message to send to the agent

function.name: gt_mail_inbox
function.description: List messages in the witness inbox, showing sender, subject, date, and read status.

function.name: gt_mail_read
function.description: Read the full content of a specific mail message by ID.

function.name: gt_mail_send
function.description: Send a mail message to another agent. Use for protocol messages (MERGE_READY, RECOVERY_NEEDED, ESCALATION) that must survive session death.

function.name: gt_patrol_report
function.description: Close the current patrol wisp and start a new one, recording a summary of what happened this cycle.

summary.description: Summary of the patrol cycle (what was found, actions taken)

function.name: gt_handoff
function.description: Hand off to a fresh session with context notes. Use when context is high or after extraordinary actions.

message.description: Context notes for the next session

function.name: gt_escalate
function.description: Escalate an issue to the Mayor with a severity level.

message.description: Description of the issue being escalated

function.name: bd_show
function.description: View details of a bead (issue/wisp) including status, description, assignee, and labels.

function.name: bd_list
function.description: List beads matching filter criteria (status, type, label, assignee).

type.description: Filter by type (task, epic, agent, merge-request, timer)

function.name: bd_children
function.description: List child beads of an epic/parent bead.

function.name: check_git_state
function.description: Check a polecat's git repository state (uncommitted changes, unpushed commits).

polecat_path.description: Path to the polecat's git worktree

function.name: check_tmux_session
function.description: Check if specific tmux sessions are alive (deacon, refinery, etc.).

PATROL_IDLE.description: No polecats active, all infrastructure healthy. No action needed.
PATROL_IDLE.outcome: Close patrol, create new patrol wisp

NUDGE.description: Polecat appears stuck or idle. Send a wake-up message.

ESCALATE.description: Repeated nudges failed, crash loop detected, or work cannot be recovered.

NUKE.description: Polecat in broken state, git clean, safe to kill.
NUKE.outcome: Session killed, worktree deleted, agent bead closed

FORCE_NUKE.description: Polecat in broken state, repeated failures, override safety checks.
FORCE_NUKE.outcome: Forced kill, escalation to Mayor for work recovery

MERGE_READY.description: Polecat completed work, git state clean, notify refinery before cleanup.
MERGE_READY.outcome: Refinery notified, polecat can be nuked after

RECOVER.description: Polecat has unpushed work that needs saving before cleanup.
RECOVER.outcome: Mayor notified for recovery, no nuke

HANDOFF.description: Context high or extraordinary action taken. Hand off to fresh session.
HANDOFF.outcome: Session ends, next session picks up from hook
